function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# Hermes Agent - Development Guide

Instructions for AI coding assistants and developers working on the hermes-agent codebase.

## Development Environment

```bash
source venv/bin/activate  # ALWAYS activate before running Python
```

## Project Structure

```
hermes-agent/
├── run_agent.py          # AIAgent class — core conversation loop
├── model_tools.py        # Tool orchestration, _discover_tools(), handle_function_call()
├── toolsets.py           # Toolset definitions, _HERMES_CORE_TOOLS list
├── cli.py                # HermesCLI class — interactive CLI orchestrator
├── hermes_state.py       # SessionDB — SQLite session store (FTS5 search)
├── agent/                # Agent internals
│   ├── prompt_builder.py     # System prompt assembly
│   ├── context_compressor.py # Auto context compression
│   ├── prompt_caching.py     # Anthropic prompt caching
│   ├── auxiliary_client.py   # Auxiliary LLM client (vision, summarization)
│   ├── model_metadata.py     # Model context lengths, token estimation
│   ├── models_dev.py         # models.dev registry integration (provider-aware context)
│   ├── display.py            # KawaiiSpinner, tool preview formatting
│   ├── skill_commands.py     # Skill slash commands (shared CLI/gateway)
│   └── trajectory.py         # Trajectory saving helpers

├── acp_adapter/          # ACP server (VS Code / Zed / JetBrains integration)

**User config:** `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (settings), `~/.hermes/.env` (API keys)

## File Dependency Chain

```
tools/registry.py  (no deps — imported by all tool files)
       ↑
tools/*.py  (each calls registry.register() at import time)
       ↑
model_tools.py  (imports tools/registry + triggers tool discovery)
       ↑
run_agent.py, cli.py, batch_runner.py, environments/
```

---

## AIAgent Class (run_agent.py)

```python
class AIAgent:
    def __init__(self,
        model: str = "anthropic/claude-opus-4.6",
        max_iterations: int = 90,
        enabled_toolsets: list = None,
        disabled_toolsets: list = None,
        quiet_mode: bool = False,
        save_trajectories: bool = False,
        platform: str = None,           # "cli", "telegram", etc.
        session_id: str = None,
        skip_context_files: bool = False,
        skip_memory: bool = False,
        # ... plus provider, api_mode, callbacks, routing params
    ): ...

    def chat(self, message: str) -> str:
        """Simple interface — returns final response string."""

    def run_conversation(self, user_message: str, system_message: str = None,
                         conversation_history: list = None, task_id: str = None) -> dict:
        """Full interface — returns dict with final_response + messages."""
```

### Agent Loop

The core loop is inside `run_conversation()` — entirely synchronous:

```python
while api_call_count < self.max_iterations and self.iteration_budget.remaining > 0:
    response = client.chat.completions.create(model=model, messages=messages, tools=tool_schemas)
    if response.tool_calls:
        for tool_call in response.tool_calls:
            result = handle_function_call(tool_call.name, tool_call.args, task_id)
            messages.append(tool_result_message(result))
        api_call_count += 1
    else:
        return response.content
```

Messages follow OpenAI format: `{"role": "system/user/assistant/tool", ...}`. Reasoning content is stored in `assistant_msg["reasoning"]`.

---

## CLI Architecture (cli.py)

- **Rich** for banner/panels, **prompt_toolkit** for input with autocomplete
- **KawaiiSpinner** (`agent/display.py`) — animated faces during API calls, `┊` activity feed for tool results
- `load_cli_config()` in cli.py merges hardcoded defaults + user config YAML
- **Skin engine** (`hermes_cli/skin_engine.py`) — data-driven CLI theming; initialized from `display.skin` config key at startup; skins customize banner colors, spinner faces/verbs/wings, tool prefix, response box, branding text
- `process_command()` is a method on `HermesCLI` — dispatches on canonical command name resolved via `resolve_command()` from the central registry
- Skill slash commands: `agent/skill_commands.py` scans `~/.hermes/skills/`, injects as **user message** (not system prompt) to preserve prompt caching

### Slash Command Registry (`hermes_cli/commands.py`)

All slash commands are defined in a central `COMMAND_REGISTRY` list of `CommandDef` objects. Every downstream consumer derives from this registry automatically:

- **CLI** — `process_command()` resolves aliases via `resolve_command()`, dispatches on canonical name
- **Gateway** — `GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS` frozenset for hook emission, `resolve_command()` for dispatch
- **Gateway help** — `gateway_help_lines()` generates `/help` output
- **Telegram** — `telegram_bot_commands()` generates the BotCommand menu
- **Slack** — `slack_subcommand_map()` generates `/hermes` subcommand routing
- **Autocomplete** — `COMMANDS` flat dict feeds `SlashCommandCompleter`
- **CLI help** — `COMMANDS_BY_CATEGORY` dict feeds `show_help()`

### Adding a Slash Command

1. Add a `CommandDef` entry to `COMMAND_REGISTRY` in `hermes_cli/commands.py`:
```python
CommandDef("mycommand", "Description of what it does", "Session",
           aliases=("mc",), args_hint="[arg]"),
```
2. Add handler in `HermesCLI.process_command()` in `cli.py`:
```python
elif canonical == "mycommand":
    self._handle_mycommand(cmd_original)
```
3. If the command is available in the gateway, add a handler in `gateway/run.py`:
```python
if canonical == "mycommand":
    return await self._handle_mycommand(event)
```
4. For persistent settings, use `save_config_value()` in `cli.py`

**CommandDef fields:**
- `name` — canonical name without slash (e.g. `"background"`)
- `description` — human-readable description
- `category` — one of `"Session"`, `"Confi

{"content": "     1|# Orchestrator Daemon
     2|
     3|## What this is
     4|
     5|The orchestrator daemon is a background loop that runs inside the Hermes
     6|Railway service.  It turns Hermes from a chat-only Telegram bot into the
     7|brain of the Lawyer Incorporated paralegal stack — the single point of
     8|coordination that decides what work needs doing, assigns it to the right
     9|OpenClaw agent, watches for results, and makes sure a human reviews
    10|everything before it goes anywhere.
    11|
    12|It runs as a thread alongside the Hermes gateway.  The gateway keeps
    13|handling Telegram messages normally.  The daemon runs next to it,
    14|independently, on a configurable heartbeat timer.
    15|
    16|## Why it exists
    17|
    18|The paralegal stack has three layers:
    19|
    20|1. **Hermes** (this service, Railway) — the orchestrator.  One instance.
    21|   Knows the big picture.  Talks to you on Telegram.  Decides what needs
    22|   doing and who should do it.
    23|
    24|2. **OpenClaw agents** (Render) — the workers.  Many instances, each
    25|   potentially specialized (document review, research, drafting, etc.).
    26|   They have their own heartbeat and lifecycle managed by the OpenClaw
    27|   gateway.  They do the actual paralegal work.
    28|
    29|3. **Mission Control / FirmVault** (not yet built) — the task queue and
    30|   document store.  Mission Control holds the queue of tasks that need
    31|   orchestrating and the approval pipeline for completed work.  FirmVault
    32|   holds documents and evidence.
    33|
    34|Without the daemon, Hermes only responds when you message it.  With the
    35|daemon, Hermes proactively checks for work, assigns it out, collects
    36|results, and routes them for your review — all in the background, whether
    37|or not you're actively chatting with it.
    38|
    39|## How it fits together
    40|
    41|```
    42|                     ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
    43|                     │  Railway Service (Hermes = Orchestrator) │
    44|                     │                                          │
    45|                     │  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  │
    46|                     │  │ Hermes       │  │ Orchestrator     │  │
    47|                     │  │ Gateway      │  │ Daemon (thread)  │  │
    48|                     │  │ (Telegram,   │  │                  │  │
    49|                     │  │  messaging)  │  │ health check     │  │
    50|                     │  │              │  │ poll tasks       │  │
    51|                     │  │              │  │ delegate → agents│  │
    52|                     │  │              │  │ collect results  │  │
    53|                     │  │              │  │ route → approval │  │
    54|                     │  └──────────────┘  └────────┬─────────┘  │
    55|                     │                             │            │
    56|                     └─────────────────────────────┼────────────┘
    57|                                                   │
    58|                  ┌────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────┐
    59|                  │                                │                  │
    60|                  ▼                                ▼                  ▼
    61|   ┌───────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐
    62|   │  OpenClaw Gateway     │      │  Mission Control │  │  FirmVault       │
    63|   │  (Render)             │      │  (TODO)          │  │  (TODO)          │
    64|   │                       │      │  task queue +    │  │  document store  │
    65|   │  ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐     │      │  approval queue  │  │                  │
    66|   │  │Agent│ │Agent│ ... │      └──────────────────┘  └──────────────────┘
    67|   │  │  1  │ │  2  │     │
    68|   │  └─────┘ └─────┘     │
    69|   │  (own heartbeat)      │
    70|   └───────────────────────┘
    71|```
    72|
    73|The direction of control flows **from Hermes outward**:
    74|
    75|- Hermes asks Mission Control: "Any tasks I need to handle?"
    76|- Hermes asks OpenClaw: "Which agents are free?"
    77|- Hermes tells OpenClaw: "Give this task to Agent 2."
    78|- Hermes asks OpenClaw: "Any results back yet?"
    79|- Hermes tells Mission Control: "Here's the result — put it in the review queue."
    80|
    81|OpenClaw agents never call Hermes.  They only talk to the OpenClaw gateway,
    82|which manages their own heartbeat and lifecycle independently.
    83|
    84|## What happens on each tick
    85|
    86|Every `DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS` (default 60), the daemon runs one cycle:
    87|
    88|**1. Health check**
    89|
    90|Pings the OpenClaw gateway (and Mission Control / FirmVault if configured).
    91|If OpenClaw is unreachable, the tick stops early — there's no point trying
    92|to delegate work if the agents can't be reached.
    93|
    94|**2. Poll for tasks**
    95|
    96|Asks Mission Control for tasks that need orchestrating.  These are tasks
    97|that have been created (by you, by a scheduled job, by another system) but
    98|haven't been assigned to an agent yet.
    99|
   100|Right now Mission Control doesn't exist, so this returns an empty list and
   101|the daemon logs "no tasks available" and moves on.
   102|
   103|**3. Delegate to agents**
   104|
   105|For each task found, the daemon asks OpenClaw for available (idle) agents,
   106|picks one that matches the task type, and sends the assignment.  The agent
   107|starts working.
   108|
   109|Right now the agent listing and delegation endpoints don't exist on
   110|OpenClaw, so this is a no-op.
   111|
   112|**4. Collect results**
   113|
   114|Asks OpenClaw for any completed work from agents.  If an agent finished a
   115|task since the last tick, its result shows up here.
   116|
   117|**5. Route to approval**
   118|
   119|This is the critical safety step.  Every result is tagged `pending_review`
   120|and submitted to the approval pipeline.  In testing mode
   121|(`DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY=true`, the default), **nothing is ever auto-accepted**.
   122|A human must explicitly approve each piece of work before it goes anywhere.
   123|
   124|If Mission Control isn't configured yet, the result is logged locally at
   125|WARNING level so it's impossible to miss in the Railway deploy logs.
   126|
   127|## How it starts and stops
   128|
   129|The daemon uses the Hermes gateway's hook system.  When the gateway boots,
   130|it emits a `gateway:startup` event.  A built-in hook
   131|(`gateway/builtin_hooks/worker_daemon.py`) catches this event and, if
   132|`DAEMON_ENABLED=true`, spawns a background thread running the supervisor
   133|loop.
   134|
   135|- **Startup**: gateway boots → hook fires → daemon thread starts → waits
   136|  `DAEMON_INITIAL_DELAY_SECONDS` (default 10) for the gateway to finish
   137|  booting → begins ticking.
   138|- **Shutdown**: you press Ctrl+C (or Railway stops the container) → SIGINT
   139|  arrives → gateway calls `runner.stop()` → the daemon thread is a daemon
   140|  thread, so Python kills it automatically when the main thread exits.
   141|  The daemon also checks a `threading.Event` between ticks, so it can stop
   142|  mid-sleep without waiting for the full heartbeat interval.
   143|- **Duplicate prevention**: a module-level `threading.Lock` ensures only one
   144|  daemon instance runs per process.  A second call to `start()` is rejected.
   145|
   146|The daemon creates its own asyncio event loop (the gateway owns the main
   147|one).  Errors in the daemon never crash the gateway — they're caught,
   148|logged, and the next tick proceeds normally.
   149|
   150|## Safety guarantees
   151|
   152|This system is designed for a legal context where mistakes have real
   153|consequences.  The safety model is:
   154|
   155|1. **Off by default.**  `DAEMON_ENABLED` defaults to `false`.  You must
   156|   explicitly turn it on.
   157|
   158|2. **Approval-only by default.**  `DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY` defaults to `true`.
   159|   Every result from every agent is forced into `pending_review` status
   160|   regardless of what the agent or the queue says.  There is no code path
   161|   that auto-accepts work when this flag is true.
   162|
   163|3. **Errors don't propagate.**  If the OpenClaw gateway goes down, the
   164|   daemon logs a warning and sleeps until the next tick.  If Mission
   165|   Control returns garbage, the daemon catches the exception and moves on.
   166|   The Hermes gateway (and your Telegram bot) keeps running regardless.
   167|
   168|4. **Single writer.**  `railway.toml` enforces `numReplicas = 1`, and
   169|   the module-level lock prevents duplicate loops within a process.
   170|   There is no distributed lock for multi-replica scenarios — this is
   171|   documented as a known limitation, not papered over.
   172|
   173|## Environment variables
   174|
   175|| Variable | Default | What it does |
   176||---|---|---|
   177|| `DAEMON_ENABLED` | `false` | Master on/off switch. |
   178|| `DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS` | `60` | Time between ticks. Lower = more responsive, higher = less load. |
   179|| `DAEMON_INITIAL_DELAY_SECONDS` | `10` | Pause before the first tick so the gateway can finish booting. |
   180|| `DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY` | `true` | Forces all results to pending_review. Leave true during testing. |
   181|| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL` | `https://openclaw-gateway-dfdi.onrender.com` | Where to reach the OpenClaw gateway. |
   182|| `MISSION_CONTROL_URL` | *(empty)* | Where to reach Mission Control. Adapter disabled when empty. |
   183|| `FIRMVAULT_URL` | *(empty)* | Where to reach FirmVault. Adapter disabled when empty. |
   184|| `DAEMON_WORKER_ID` | *(auto-generated)* | Unique identity for this Hermes instance. Used to tag results. |
   185|| `DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL` | `INFO` | Controls verbosity of daemon-specific logs. |
   186|| `DAEMON_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | `10` | HTTP timeout for health probes. |
   187|
   188|## Enabling it on Railway
   189|
   190|Add these to your Railway service's **Variables** tab (alongside the
   191|existing `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, etc.):
   192|
   193|```
   194|DAEMON_ENABLED=true
   195|DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS=60
   196|DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY=true
   197|OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=https://openclaw-gateway-dfdi.onrender.com
   198|```
   199|
   200|Railway redeploys automatically.  In the **Deploy Logs** you'll see:
   201|
   202|```
   203|worker daemon hook: starting supervisor (worker_id=a3f8c1b2)
   204|orchestrator started — worker_id=a3f8c1b2 heartbeat=60s approval_only=True
   205|orchestrator: initial delay 10s before first tick
   206|heartbeat tick=1 orchestrator=a3f8c1b2
   207|health: openclaw OK (245ms)
   208|poll: no tasks available from any source
   209|heartbeat tick=2 orchestrator=a3f8c1b2
   210|health: openclaw OK (198ms)
   211|poll: no tasks available from any source
   212|...
   213|```
   214|
   215|This is normal — there are no tasks because Mission Control doesn't exist
   216|yet.  The daemon is alive, healthy, and ready.  Once you wire up the task
   217|queue, it'll start delegating.
   218|
   219|## Running locally
   220|
   221|```bash
   222|export DAEMON_ENABLED=true
   223|export DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS=10   # faster for local dev
   224|export DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY=true
   225|hermes gateway run
   226|```
   227|
   228|Watch the console for lines prefixed with `daemon.` loggers.  Ctrl+C stops
   229|both the gateway and the daemon.
   230|
   231|## What's built vs. what's stubbed
   232|
   233|| Component | Status | Notes |
   234||---|---|---|
   235|| Daemon lifecycle (start, stop, heartbeat, duplicate prevention) | **Done** | Fully working |
   236|| Health check (OpenClaw gateway) | **Done** | Probes /health, /api/health, / |
   237|| Health check (Mission Control, FirmVault) | **Interface only** | Returns "not configured" until URLs are set |
   238|| Task polling from Mission Control | **Interface only** | Returns empty list; needs Mission Control API |
   239|| Agent listing from OpenClaw | **Interface only** | Returns empty list; needs OpenClaw endpoint |
   240|| Task delegation to OpenClaw agents | **Interface only** | No-op; needs OpenClaw endpoint |
   241|| Result collection from OpenClaw agents | **Interface only** | Returns empty list; needs OpenClaw endpoint |
   242|| Approval routing (local fallback) | **Done** | Logs results at WARNING level |
   243|| Approval routing (Mission Control) | **Interface only** | Needs Mission Control API |
   244|| FirmVault document read/write | **Interface only** | Needs FirmVault API |
   245|| Config from env vars | **Done** | All vars listed above |
   246|| Logging | **Done** | Every state transition logged |
   247|| Graceful shutdown | **Done** | Responds to SIGINT/SIGTERM within one tick |
   248|| Duplicate prevention (same process) | **Done** | Module-level threading.Lock |
   249|| Duplicate prevention (multiple replicas) | **Not implemented** | Documented limitation |
   250|| Tests | **Done** | 27 tests covering config, lifecycle, polling, routing |
   251|

   256|
   257|## Module structure
   258|

   260|daemon/

   262|├── config.py                Env var parsing → frozen DaemonConfig dataclass

{"content": "     1|"""Built-in hook — start the worker daemon on gateway startup.
     2|
     3|Follows the same pattern as boot_md.py: spawns a daemon thread that runs
     4|alongside the gateway without blocking startup.  The daemon is only started
     5|when DAEMON_ENABLED=true is set in the environment.
     6|"""
     7|
     8|from __future__ import annotations
     9|
    10|import logging
    11|
    12|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.hook")
    13|
    14|
    15|async def handle(event_type: str, context: dict) -> None:
    16|    """``gateway:startup`` handler — conditionally starts the worker daemon."""
    17|    # Late import so the daemon package is only loaded when the hook fires.
    18|    from daemon.config import DaemonConfig
    19|    from daemon.supervisor import start
    20|
    21|    config = DaemonConfig()
    22|
    23|    if not config.enabled:
    24|        logger.debug("worker daemon hook: DAEMON_ENABLED is not true — skipping")
    25|        return
    26|
    27|    logger.info("worker daemon hook: starting supervisor (worker_id=%s)", config.worker_id)
    28|    thread = start(config)
    29|    if thread is None:
    30|        logger.warning("worker daemon hook: supervisor did not start (already running or disabled)")
    31|    else:
    32|        logger.info("worker daemon hook: supervisor thread started — name=%s", thread.name)

{"content": "     1|"""FirmVault Case Pipeline adapter — Lane 1 of the Lawyer Inc architecture.
     2|
     3|Runs the FirmVault deterministic engine on each daemon tick:
     4|  1. Syncs the FirmVault repo (git pull)
     5|  2. Runs engine.assess_portfolio() to find available work across all cases
     6|  3. Runs mc_bridge push to create MC tasks for unsatisfied landmarks
     7|  4. Runs mc_bridge pull to update state.yaml from MC completed tasks
     8|  5. Detects phase transitions and logs them
     9|
    10|This adapter does NOT use GSD. The FirmVault case pipeline runs directly
    11|through Mission Control → OpenClaw agents → FirmVault commits.
    12|
    13|Environment variables:
    14|  FIRMVAULT_DIR     — path to FirmVault repo clone (default: /opt/data/firmvault)
    15|  MC_URL            — Mission Control backend URL
    16|  MC_TOKEN          — Mission Control LOCAL_AUTH_TOKEN
    17|  MC_BOARD_ID       — Default board UUID for case tasks
    18|  FIRMVAULT_REPO    — Git remote URL for FirmVault (for pull/push)
    19|"""
    20|
    21|from __future__ import annotations
    22|
    23|import json
    24|import logging
    25|import os
    26|import subprocess
    27|import sys
    28|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    29|from pathlib import Path
    30|from typing import Optional
    31|
    32|from daemon.adapters.base import HealthCheckable, HealthStatus
    33|
    34|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.firmvault")
    35|
    36|
    37|@dataclass
    38|class PortfolioSnapshot:
    39|    """Summary of engine assessment for logging."""
    40|    total_cases: int = 0
    41|    total_available_work: int = 0
    42|    total_in_flight: int = 0
    43|    transitions_ready: int = 0
    44|    errors: list = field(default_factory=list)
    45|
    46|
    47|class FirmVaultAdapter(HealthCheckable):
    48|    """Drives the FirmVault case pipeline on each daemon tick.
    49|
    50|    Unlike other adapters that implement TaskSource, this adapter
    51|    manages its own complete loop: engine → mc_bridge → state updates.
    52|    It doesn't feed tasks into the daemon's generic poll/delegate cycle
    53|    because the FirmVault→MC→OpenClaw pipeline is self-contained.
    54|    """
    55|
    56|    def __init__(self) -> None:
    57|        self.vault_dir = Path(
    58|            os.environ.get("FIRMVAULT_DIR", "/opt/data/firmvault")
    59|        )
    60|        self.runtime_dir = self.vault_dir / "skills.tools.workflows" / "runtime"
    61|        self.mc_url = os.environ.get("MC_URL", "")
    62|        self.mc_api_key = os.environ.get("MC_API_KEY", os.environ.get("MC_TOKEN", ""))
    63|        self.repo_url = os.environ.get(
    64|            "FIRMVAULT_REPO",
    65|            "https://github.com/Whaleylaw/FirmVault.git"
    66|        )
    67|
    68|    @property
    69|    def configured(self) -> bool:
    70|        """True if FirmVault repo and MC credentials are available."""
    71|        return (
    72|            self.vault_dir.exists()
    73|            and (self.runtime_dir / "engine.py").exists()
    74|            and bool(self.mc_url)
    75|            and bool(self.mc_api_key)
    76|        )
    77|
    78|    # ── HealthCheckable ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    79|
    80|    async def health_check(self) -> HealthStatus:
    81|        if not self.vault_dir.exists():
    82|            return HealthStatus(
    83|                service_name="firmvault",
    84|                healthy=False,
    85|                latency_ms=0,
    86|                error="FirmVault repo not cloned",
    87|            )
    88|
    89|        if not (self.runtime_dir / "engine.py").exists():
    90|            return HealthStatus(
    91|                service_name="firmvault",
    92|                healthy=False,
    93|                latency_ms=0,
    94|                error="FirmVault runtime not found at expected path",
    95|            )
    96|
    97|        if not self.mc_url or not self.mc_api_key:
    98|            return HealthStatus(
    99|                service_name="firmvault",
   100|                healthy=False,
   101|                latency_ms=0,
   102|                error="MC_URL or MC_TOKEN not configured",
   103|            )
   104|
   105|        return HealthStatus(
   106|            service_name="firmvault",
   107|            healthy=True,
   108|            latency_ms=0,
   109|            error=None,
   110|        )
   111|
   112|    # ── Main tick ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113|
   114|    async def tick(self) -> None:
   115|        """Run one full Lane 1 cycle.
   116|
   117|        Called from the supervisor on each heartbeat. This is the complete
   118|        FirmVault → MC → state update pipeline.
   119|        """
   120|        if not self.configured:
   121|            logger.debug("firmvault: not configured, skipping tick")
   122|            return
   123|
   124|        # Step 1: Git pull to get latest vault state
   125|        self._git_sync()
   126|
   127|        # Step 2: Run engine to assess portfolio

   135|            snapshot.total_available_work,
   136|            snapshot.total_in_flight,
   137|            snapshot.transitions_ready,
   138|        )
   139|
   140|        if snapshot.errors:

   148|        # Step 4: Pull completed tasks from MC and update state.yaml

   154|    # ── Internal helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   155|
   156|    def _git_sync(self) -> None:

   166|
   167|    def _git_commit_and_push(self) -> None:
   168|        """Commit and push any state.yaml changes made by mc_bridge pull."""
   169|        result = self._run_shell(

   184|                cwd=str(self.vault_dir),

   187|
   188|    def _run_engine(self) -> Optional[PortfolioSnapshot]:
   189|        """Run the FirmVault engine to assess all cases."""
   190|        script = f"""
   191|import sys, json
   192|sys.path.insert(0, '{self.runtime_dir}')
   193|from engine import PhaseDag, Engine
   194|
   195|dag_path = '{self.vault_dir}/skills.tools.workflows/workflows/PHASE_DAG.yaml'
   196|dag = PhaseDag(dag_path)
   197|engine = Engine(dag)
   198|summary = engine.assess_portfolio('{self.vault_dir}')
   199|
   200|result = {{
   201|    'total_cases': len(summary.assessments),
   202|    'total_available_work': summary.total_available_work,
   203|    'total_in_flight': summary.total_in_flight,
   204|    'transitions_ready': len(summary.transitions_ready),
   205|    'errors': summary.errors[:10],
   206|    'available_by_case': {{
   207|        a.case_slug: len(a.available_work)
   208|        for a in summary.assessments
   209|        if a.available_work
   210|    }},
   211|    'transitions': [
   212|        {{'case': t.case_slug, 'from': t.from_phase, 'to': t.to_phase, 'reason': t.reason}}
   213|        for t in summary.transitions_ready
   214|    ],
   215|}}
   216|print(json.dumps(result))
   217|"""
   218|        result = self._run_python(script)

   221|
   222|        snap = PortfolioSnapshot(

   240|
   241|        return snap

   243|    def _mc_bridge_push(self) -> None:

   286|    def _run_python(self, script: str) -> Optional[dict]:

{"content": "     1|"""Supervisor — the Hermes orchestrator daemon loop.
     2|
     3|Hermes is the orchestrator; OpenClaw agents are the workers.  On each
     4|heartbeat tick this supervisor:
     5|
     6|  1. Health-checks upstream services (OpenClaw gateway, Mission Control, FirmVault).
     7|  2. Polls Mission Control for tasks that need orchestrating.
     8|  3. Delegates tasks to available OpenClaw agents via the gateway.
     9|  4. Collects completed results from OpenClaw agents.
    10|  5. Routes results to approval/review (never auto-accepts during testing).
    11|
    12|Runs in a daemon thread alongside the Hermes gateway.
    13|"""
    14|
    15|from __future__ import annotations
    16|
    17|import asyncio
    18|import logging
    19|import threading
    20|import time
    21|from typing import Optional
    22|
    23|from daemon.adapters.base import ApprovalRouter, HealthCheckable, TaskSource
    24|from daemon.adapters.firmvault_pipeline import FirmVaultAdapter
    25|from daemon.adapters.gsd import GSDAdapter
    26|from daemon.adapters.mission_control import MissionControlAdapter
    27|from daemon.adapters.openclaw import OpenClawAdapter
    28|from daemon.approval import LocalApprovalRouter
    29|from daemon.config import DaemonConfig
    30|from daemon.health import check_all, log_health
    31|from daemon.poller import collect_and_route_results, delegate_task, poll_for_tasks
    32|
    33|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.supervisor")
    34|
    35|# Module-level lock to prevent duplicate concurrent loops within the same
    36|# process.  A second call to start() while the first is still running will
    37|# be rejected immediately.
    38|_instance_lock = threading.Lock()
    39|_running_instance: Optional["Supervisor"] = None
    40|
    41|
    42|class Supervisor:
    43|    """Hermes orchestrator daemon — runs in a background thread."""
    44|
    45|    def __init__(self, config: DaemonConfig) -> None:
    46|        self.config = config
    47|        self._stop_event = threading.Event()
    48|        self._tick_count = 0
    49|
    50|        # ── Build adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────
    51|        self._openclaw = OpenClawAdapter(
    52|            config.openclaw_gateway_url,
    53|            timeout=config.health_check_timeout_seconds,
    54|        )
    55|        self._mission_control = MissionControlAdapter(
    56|            config.mission_control_url,
    57|            timeout=config.health_check_timeout_seconds,
    58|        )
    59|        # Lane 1: FirmVault case pipeline (engine → MC → OpenClaw → vault)
    60|        self._firmvault = FirmVaultAdapter()
    61|
    62|        # GSD adapter — drives plan lifecycle from project workspaces.
    63|        self._gsd = GSDAdapter()
    64|
    65|        # Services to health-check (skip unconfigured ones to avoid noise).
    66|        self._health_targets: list[HealthCheckable] = [self._openclaw]
    67|        if self._mission_control.configured:
    68|            self._health_targets.append(self._mission_control)
    69|        if self._firmvault.configured:
    70|            self._health_targets.append(self._firmvault)
    71|        if self._gsd.configured:
    72|            self._health_targets.append(self._gsd)
    73|
    74|        # Task sources to poll for orchestration work.
    75|        self._task_sources: list[TaskSource] = []
    76|        if self._mission_control.configured:
    77|            self._task_sources.append(self._mission_control)
    78|        if self._gsd.configured:
    79|            self._task_sources.append(self._gsd)
    80|
    81|        # Approval router: prefer Mission Control if configured, otherwise
    82|        # fall back to local logging-based router.
    83|        self._approval_router: ApprovalRouter
    84|        if self._mission_control.configured:
    85|            self._approval_router = self._mission_control
    86|        else:
    87|            self._approval_router = LocalApprovalRouter()
    88|
    89|    # ── Lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90|
    91|    def request_stop(self) -> None:
    92|        """Signal the supervisor loop to stop at the next opportunity."""
    93|        self._stop_event.set()
    94|
    95|    @property
    96|    def is_stopping(self) -> bool:
    97|        return self._stop_event.is_set()
    98|
    99|    # ── Main loop (runs in a daemon thread) ───────────────────────────
   100|
   101|    def run(self) -> None:
   102|        """Blocking entry point.  Call from a daemon thread."""
   103|        logger.info(
   104|            "orchestrator started — worker_id=%s heartbeat=%ds approval_only=%s",
   105|            self.config.worker_id,
   106|            self.config.heartbeat_seconds,
   107|            self.config.approval_only,
   108|        )
   109|        logger.info("orchestrator config: %s", self.config.summary())
   110|
   111|        # Initial delay to let the gateway finish booting.
   112|        if self.config.initial_delay_seconds > 0:
   113|            logger.info(
   114|                "orchestrator: initial delay %ds before first tick",
   115|                self.config.initial_delay_seconds,
   116|            )
   117|            if self._stop_event.wait(timeout=self.config.initial_delay_seconds):
   118|                logger.info("orchestrator: stop requested during initial delay")
   119|                return
   120|
   121|        # Create a dedicated event loop for this thread (the gateway owns
   122|        # the main loop; we must not share it).
   123|        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
   124|        try:
   125|            while not self._stop_event.is_set():
   126|                self._tick_count += 1
   127|                t0 = time.monotonic()
   128|                try:
   129|                    loop.run_until_complete(self._tick())
   130|                except Exception as exc:
   131|                    logger.error(
   132|                        "orchestrator: unhandled error in tick %d — %s",
   133|                        self._tick_count, exc,
   134|                        exc_info=True,
   135|                    )
   136|                elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
   137|                logger.debug(
   138|                    "orchestrator: tick %d completed in %.1fs",
   139|                    self._tick_count, elapsed,
   140|                )
   141|
   142|                # Sleep until the next heartbeat, but wake early on stop.
   143|                remaining = max(0, self.config.heartbeat_seconds - elapsed)
   144|                if remaining > 0:
   145|                    self._stop_event.wait(timeout=remaining)
   146|        finally:
   147|            loop.close()
   148|            logger.info(
   149|                "orchestrator stopped after %d ticks — worker_id=%s",
   150|                self._tick_count, self.config.worker_id,
   151|            )
   152|
   153|    # ── Single tick ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   154|
   155|    async def _tick(self) -> None:
   156|        """One heartbeat cycle.
   157|
   158|        Two lanes run in parallel:
   159|          Lane 1: FirmVault case pipeline (engine → MC → OpenClaw → vault)
   160|          Lane 2: GSD ad-hoc projects (poll → dispatch → collect)
   161|        """
   162|        logger.info(
   163|            "heartbeat tick=%d orchestrator=%s",
   164|            self._tick_count, self.config.worker_id,
   165|        )
   166|
   167|        # ── Lane 1: FirmVault case pipeline ──────────────────────────
   168|        # Runs the deterministic engine + MC bridge. Self-contained loop.
   169|        if self._firmvault.configured:
   170|            try:
   171|                await self._firmvault.tick()
   172|            except Exception as exc:
   173|                logger.error("lane1: FirmVault tick error — %s", exc, exc_info=True)
   174|
   175|        # ── MC heartbeat ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   176|        if self._mission_control.configured:
   177|            try:
   178|                await self._mission_control.send_heartbeat()
   179|            except Exception as exc:
   180|                logger.debug("mc heartbeat error: %s", exc)
   181|
   182|        # ── Lane 2: GSD + OpenClaw orchestration ────────────────────
   183|        # 1. Health check upstream services.
   184|        statuses = await check_all(self._health_targets)
   185|        all_healthy = log_health(statuses)
   186|
   187|        openclaw_healthy = any(
   188|            s.healthy for s in statuses if s.service_name == "openclaw"
   189|        )
   190|        if not openclaw_healthy:
   191|            logger.warning("heartbeat: OpenClaw unhealthy — skipping orchestration")
   192|            return
   193|
   194|        # 2. Poll task sources (Mission Control, GSD) for work to orchestrate.
   195|        tasks = await poll_for_tasks(self._task_sources)
   196|
   197|        # 3. Route tasks — GSD tasks dispatch through GSD, others through OpenClaw.
   198|        gsd_tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.metadata.get("source") == "gsd"]
   199|        openclaw_tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.metadata.get("source") != "gsd"]
   200|
   201|        # 3a. GSD tasks: dispatch via GSD dispatcher (which routes to
   202|        #     OpenClaw relay or Hermes directly).
   203|        if gsd_tasks:
   204|            await self._dispatch_gsd_tasks(gsd_tasks)
   205|
   206|        # 3b. Non-GSD tasks: delegate to OpenClaw agents directly.
   207|        for task in openclaw_tasks:
   208|            await delegate_task(task, self._openclaw, self.config.worker_id)
   209|
   210|        # 4. Collect completed results from OpenClaw agents.
   211|        processed = await collect_and_route_results(
   212|            self._openclaw,
   213|            self._approval_router,
   214|            self.config.worker_id,
   215|            approval_only=self.config.approval_only,
   216|        )
   217|        if processed > 0:
   218|            logger.info("heartbeat: processed %d result(s) from OpenClaw agents", processed)
   219|
   220|
   221|    # ── GSD dispatch ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222|
   223|    async def _dispatch_gsd_tasks(self, tasks: list) -> None:

   225|
   226|        GSD tasks carry their full task definition in metadata['gsd_task'].
   227|        We group by project and dispatch via the bridge, which routes each
   228|        task to the correct platform (OpenClaw relay or Hermes).
   229|        """
   230|        # Group by project
   231|        by_project: dict[str, list] = {}
   232|        for task in tasks:
   233|            project = task.metadata.get("project", "unknown")
   234|            by_project.setdefault(project, []).append(task)
   235|
   236|        for project, project_tasks in by_project.items():
   237|            # Separate approval tasks from dispatch tasks
   238|            approval_tasks = [t for t in project_tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.PENDING_REVIEW]
   239|            dispatch_tasks = [t for t in project_tasks if t.status != TaskStatus.PENDING_REVIEW]
   240|
   241|            # Handle approval notifications
   242|            for task in approval_tasks:
   243|                gsd_task = task.metadata.get("gsd_task", {})
   244|                logger.warning(
   245|                    "GSD APPROVAL NEEDED: project=%s task=%s title='%s' — "
   246|                    "awaiting Aaron's approval",
   247|                    project, gsd_task.get("id"), gsd_task.get("title"),
   248|                )
   249|                # TODO: Send Telegram notification to Aaron
   250|                # This will be wired once we have the gateway's send_message hook
   251|
   252|            # Dispatch ready tasks via GSD bridge
   253|            if dispatch_tasks:
   254|                wave = dispatch_tasks[0].metadata.get("wave", 1)
   255|                gsd_payloads = [t.metadata.get("gsd_task", {}) for t in dispatch_tasks]

   260|                        "wave": wave,
   261|                        "tasks": gsd_payloads,
   262|                        "dryRun": False,
   263|                    })
   264|
   265|                    if result.get("ok"):
   266|                        logger.info(
   267|                            "gsd: dispatched wave %d for project %s — %d tasks",
   268|                            wave, project, len(dispatch_tasks),
   269|                        )
   270|                        # Update statuses
   271|                        for task in dispatch_tasks:
   272|                            gsd_task_id = task.metadata.get("gsd_task", {}).get("id")
   273|                            if gsd_task_id:
   274|                                await self._gsd.update_task_status(
   275|                                    f"{project}:{gsd_task_id}",
   276|                                    TaskStatus.ASSIGNED,
   277|                                )
   278|                    else:
   279|                        logger.error(
   280|                            "gsd: dispatch failed for project %s wave %d — %s",
   281|                            project, wave, result.get("error"),

   288|
   289|
   290|# ── Module-level start/stop helpers ──────────────────────────────────

# FirmVault — Agent Orientation

> **Read this in full at the start of every session.** It names the thesis, the non-negotiable rules, and where to find the authoritative docs. Short deliberately — use the imports below for detail.

## What this repo is

FirmVault is the **development environment for an AI paralegal backend** intended to eventually be sold to other personal-injury law firms. This specific repo is Whaley Law Firm's own instance; the architecture we prove here becomes the product.

Two parallel product tracks exist:

1. **This repo** — the GitHub-native paralegal backend: markdown vault, `PHASE_DAG.yaml`, `SKILL.md` library, runtime (materializer + worker), intake pipeline.
2. **Separate Huly fork** (not in this repo) — the lawyer-facing UI. This repo's "UI" is for operators, not lawyers.

## The thesis

**"The firm as a code repo."** Law firm work is repeatable tasks + templates + rules, which is exactly what code is. Put the firm's work into primitives agents already excel at (issues, branches, PRs, diffs) and you get a much better AI experience than any traditional PMS bolt-on.

## Core architectural principles

1. **The vault is a shadow, not the source of truth.** Real files (signed PDFs, audio, emails, faxes) live on the firm's own storage (their server, Dropbox, Box, Drive). The markdown in `cases/<slug>/` is a PHI-masked projection of those files.
2. **Intake is a pipeline, not an agent**: document arrives → real file placed on user's storage → converted to markdown → PHI stripped → opens a PR against this repo. The PR is the first human-in-the-loop gate.
3. **Git is the deliberate interface.** Every agent action is a commit or PR. Free audit trail, free approval gates, trivial rollback. Agents *never* touch real files — only their masked shadows.
4. **Markdown, not a graph DB.** We tried FalkorDB and abandoned it. LLM agents are empirically more reliable on plain text + grep than on Cypher queries. This is a deliberate choice, not laziness.
5. **PHI never lives in the vault.** Placeholders like `{{client_ssn}}` and `{{dob}}` reference secrets held in the user's own infrastructure. At render time, deterministic code (not the model) substitutes real values. The model never sees raw PII.
6. **The vault is the only state store for this layer.** No external database. No per-case JSON files. No FalkorDB. No `${ROSCOE_ROOT}`. See `@skills.tools.workflows/DATA_CONTRACT.md` for the authoritative mapping.

## Do NOT do these things

These are actively deprecated. If you see them in old code or docs, treat them as bugs.

- **Do not reference FalkorDB, Cypher, `graph_client`, `graph_manager`, or `run_cypher_query`.** All removed. Everything reads the vault.
- **Do not reference per-case JSON files** (`overview.json`, `contacts.json`, `insurance.json`, `liens.json`, `expenses.json`, `medical_providers.json`, `case_state.json`). Deleted.
- **Do not reference `${ROSCOE_ROOT}`** or any absolute-path scaffolding. Everything is repo-relative.
- **Do not edit content between `<!-- roscoe-medical-start -->` / `<!-- roscoe-medical-end -->` or `<!-- roscoe-insurance-start -->` / `<!-- roscoe-insurance-end -->` markers.** Those blocks are owned by the import scripts and will be overwritten on next sync.
- **Do not invent new vault paths.** If the thing you want to write doesn't have a home in `DATA_CONTRACT.md`, update the contract first (same commit), then write. Drift between contract and reality is the bug.
- **Do not create top-level dashboards, reports, or state files outside the vault layout**. Operator views read the vault via GitHub API or git; they don't create parallel state.

## Where the authoritative docs live

Import these at launch — they are the contract, not me paraphrasing:

@DESIGN.md

@skills.tools.workflows/DATA_CONTRACT.md

@MEMORY.md

Also non-imported but worth knowing:

- `skills.tools.workflows/workflows/PHASE_DAG.yaml` — machine-readable phase definitions, landmarks, and predicate vocabulary. The materializer reads this.
- `skills.tools.workflows/runtime/README.md` — how the materializer and worker agents drive the task queue.
- `skills.tools.workflows/runtime/task_schema.md` — the contract for task issues (labels, frontmatter, lifecycle).
- `Templates/INDEX.md` — the firm template library (73 slugified templates grouped by category).
- `skills.tools.workflows/Skills/*/SKILL.md` — 42 modernized skills in Anthropic Agent Skills format. Exemplars: `lien-management`, `deposition-prep`, `deposition-strategy`, `pip-application`.

## Slug rules (critical for wikilinks)

From `DATA_CONTRACT.md` §4:

- Lowercase
- Apostrophes and curly quotes **stripped** (no replacement char): `K'daya` → `kdaya`
- `&` → `and`
- All other non-alphanumeric runs collapsed to a single hyphen
- Leading/trailing hyphens stripped

Mismatched slugs break wikilinks across the vault. Slug before you write.

## Runtime model (what's being built)

The system is being turned from passive documentation into an active work-driving loop:

```
Vault (cases/*) ← materializer (cron) → GitHub Issues ← worker (event) → Vault
```

- **Materializer** walks open cases, reads each case's `landmarks:` frontmatter + `PHASE_DAG.yaml`, opens a GitHub Issue for every unsatisfied landmark whose task template preconditions are met. Already prototyped as a prompt in `runtime/materializer_prompt.md`.
- **Worker** is triggered by the `status:ready` label on an issue, claims it, loads the named skill, runs it, commits vault changes that reference the task_id, and flips the issue status. Prototyped in `runtime/worker_prompt.md`.
- **Source of truth** for whether a landmark is satisfied is `case.frontmatter.landmarks.<id>` — the materializer checks this first and only falls back to evaluating a predicate if the key is missing.

## Working branch

All in-progress work lives on `claude/import-cases-from-drive-xiQgL`. Do not push directly to `main` without an explicit request.

## When in doubt

- Read `DATA_CONTRACT.md` before writing to the vault.
- Read `PHASE_DAG.yaml` before touching workflow/landmark logic.
- Read `MEMORY.md` to find out what's in flight and what's pending.
- Check the last 3–5 commits on the working branch to see the most recent decisions.

    98|def get_existing_tasks() -> dict:
    99|    """Get all tasks from MC, keyed by title for dedup."""
   100|    tasks = {}
   101|    page = 1
   102|    limit = 200  # Fetch large pages to minimize requests
   103|    while True:
   104|        result = mc_request("GET", f"/api/tasks?limit={limit}&page={page}")
   105|        if "error" in result:
   106|            break
   107|        items = result.get("tasks", [])
   108|        for item in items:
   109|            tasks[item["title"]] = item
   110|        total = result.get("total", 0)
   111|        if page * limit >= total or len(items) < limit:
   112|            break
   113|        page += 1
   114|        time.sleep(2)  # Respect rate limits between pages
   115|    return tasks
   116|
   117|
   118|def task_title(case_slug: str, landmark_id: str, landmark_name: str, phase: str) -> str:
   119|    """Generate a deterministic task title for dedup."""
   120|    phase_short = PHASE_DISPLAY.get(phase, phase)
   121|    return f"[{phase_short}] {case_slug}: {landmark_name}"
   122|
   123|
   124|def push_tasks(vault_root: str, dry_run: bool = False):
   125|    """Push engine available_work to Mission Control as tasks."""
   126|    dag_path = os.path.join(vault_root, "skills.tools.workflows", "workflows", "PHASE_DAG.yaml")
   127|    dag = PhaseDag(dag_path)
   128|    engine = Engine(dag)
   129|    summary = engine.assess_portfolio(vault_root)
   130|
   131|    print(f"Engine: {summary.total_cases} cases, {summary.total_available_work} available work items")
   132|
   133|    if not dry_run:
   134|        existing = get_existing_tasks()
   135|        print(f"MC: {len(existing)} existing tasks")
   136|    else:
   137|        existing = {}
   138|
   139|    created = 0
   140|    skipped = 0
   141|
   142|    for assessment in summary.assessments:
   143|        for ul in assessment.available_work:
   144|            title = task_title(
   145|                assessment.case_slug,
   146|                ul.landmark.id,
   147|                ul.landmark.name,
   148|                assessment.current_phase,
   149|            )
   150|
   151|            if title in existing:
   152|                skipped += 1
   153|                continue
   154|
   155|            priority = "high" if ul.landmark.mandatory else "medium"
   156|            agent = PHASE_AGENT.get(assessment.current_phase)
   157|            description = (
   158|                f"**Case:** {assessment.case_slug}\n"
   159|                f"**Phase:** {assessment.phase_name}\n"
   160|                f"**Landmark:** `{ul.landmark.id}`\n"
   161|                f"**Type:** {'HARD BLOCKER' if ul.landmark.mandatory else 'Soft'}\n"
   162|                f"**Progress:** {assessment.progress['pct']}%\n"
   163|            )
   164|            if agent:
   165|                description += f"**Agent:** {agent}\n"
   166|
   167|            if dry_run:
   168|                print(f"  [DRY] Would create: {title}")
   169|                created += 1
   170|                continue
   171|
   172|            task_data = {
   173|                "title": title,
   174|                "description": description,
   175|                "status": "inbox",
   176|                "priority": priority,
   177|                "metadata": {
   178|                    "source": "firmvault-engine",
   179|                    "case_slug": assessment.case_slug,
   180|                    "landmark_id": ul.landmark.id,
   181|                    "phase": assessment.current_phase,
   182|                    "mandatory": ul.landmark.mandatory,
   183|                },
   184|            }
   185|            if agent:
   186|                task_data["assigned_to"] = agent
   187|
   188|            result = mc_request("POST", "/api/tasks", task_data)
   189|
   190|            if "error" not in result:
   191|                created += 1
   192|                # Throttle to avoid rate limits
   193|                if created % 20 == 0:
   194|                    print(f"  ... {created} created, pausing 10s")
   195|                    time.sleep(10)
   196|                else:
   197|                    time.sleep(2)
   198|            else:
   199|                print(f"  Failed: {title}")
   200|
   201|    print(f"\nPush complete: {created} created, {skipped} skipped (already exist)")
   202|
   203|    # Also report transitions
   204|    if summary.transitions_ready:
   205|        print(f"\n  PHASE TRANSITIONS READY ({len(summary.transitions_ready)}):")
   206|        for t in summary.transitions_ready:
   207|            print(f"    {t.case_slug}: {t.from_phase} → {t.to_phase}")
   208|
   209|
   210|def pull_approvals(vault_root: str, dry_run: bool = False):
   211|    """Pull done tasks from MC and update state.yaml files."""
   212|    # Fetch done tasks
   213|    done_tasks = []
   214|    page = 1
   215|    while True:
   216|        result = mc_request("GET", f"/api/tasks?status=done&limit=50&page={page}")
   217|        if "error" in result:
   218|            print(f"Error fetching tasks: {result}")
   219|            return
   220|        items = result.get("tasks", [])
   221|        done_tasks.extend(items)
   222|        if len(items) < 50:
   223|            break
   224|        page += 1
   225|
   226|    print(f"MC: {len(done_tasks)} done tasks")
   227|
   228|    updated_cases = set()
   229|    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
   230|
   231|    for task in done_tasks:
   232|        # Extract case_slug and landmark from metadata (preferred) or title
   233|        meta = task.get("metadata") or {}
   234|        case_slug = meta.get("case_slug")
   235|        landmark_id = meta.get("landmark_id")
   236|
   237|        if not case_slug or not landmark_id:
   238|            # Fall back to parsing title: [phase] case-slug: Landmark Name
   239|            title = task["title"]
   240|            try:
   241|                bracket_end = title.index("]")
   242|                rest = title[bracket_end + 2:]  # skip "] "
   243|                case_slug, landmark_name = rest.split(": ", 1)
   244|            except (ValueError, IndexError):
   245|                continue
   246|
   247|            # Try to find landmark_id from description
   248|            desc = task.get("description", "")
   249|            for line in desc.split("\n"):
   250|                if "`" in line and "Landmark" in line:
   251|                    start = line.index("`") + 1
   252|                    end = line.index("`", start)
   253|                    landmark_id = line[start:end]
   254|                    break
   255|
   256|        if not case_slug or not landmark_id:
   257|            continue
   258|
   259|        # Find and update state.yaml
   260|        state_path = os.path.join(vault_root, "cases", case_slug, "state.yaml")
   261|        if not os.path.exists(state_path):
   262|            continue
   263|
   264|        state = load_case_state(state_path)
   265|        lm = state.landmarks.get(landmark_id)
   266|
   267|        if not lm or lm.satisfied:

   269|
   270|        if dry_run:
   271|            print(f"  [DRY] Would satisfy: {case_slug}/{landmark_id}")
   272|            updated_cases.add(case_slug)
   273|            continue
   274|
   275|        # Update state.yaml
   276|        raw_lm = state.raw.get("landmarks", {}).get(landmark_id, {})
   277|        if isinstance(raw_lm, dict):
   278|            raw_lm["satisfied"] = True
   279|            raw_lm["satisfied_at"] = now
   280|            raw_lm["satisfied_by"] = f"mc:task:{task['id']}"
   281|            raw_lm["evidence"] = f"Completed in Mission Control (ticket: {task.get('ticket_ref', task['id'])})"
   282|
   283|        with open(state_path, "w") as f:
   284|            yaml.dump(state.raw, f, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False, allow_unicode=True)
   285|
   286|        updated_cases.add(case_slug)
   287|        print(f"  Updated: {case_slug}/{landmark_id}")
   288|
   289|    print(f"\nPull complete: {len(updated_cases)} cases updated")
   290|
   291|
   292|def sync(vault_root: str, dry_run: bool = False):
   293|    """Full sync: pull approvals first, then push new work."""
   294|    print("=== PULL (MC done tasks → state.yaml) ===\n")
   295|    pull_approvals(vault_root, dry_run)
   296|    print("\n=== PUSH (engine available_work → MC tasks) ===\n")
   297|    push_tasks(vault_root, dry_run)
   298|
   299|
   300|if __name__ == "__main__":
   301|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="FirmVault ↔ Mission Control bridge")
   302|    parser.add_argument("action", choices=["push", "pull", "sync"])

   304|    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")

Here is my complete analysis of the Mission Control API and how it serves as a hub for FirmVault and GSD:

---

MISSION CONTROL (MC) API ANALYSIS — WHAT IT ALREADY PROVIDES

1. WHAT MC IS

Mission Control is a Next.js app (v2.0.1) at ops.lawyerincorporated.com. It's an "AI Agent Orchestration Dashboard" — SQLite-backed, self-hosted. It serves as the central task queue, agent registry, and approval pipeline for the Lawyer Inc paralegal stack.

Auth: x-api-key header with MC_API_KEY env var.

2. DATA MODELS EXPOSED

AGENTS (/api/agents):
- 8 registered agents with fields: id, name, role, status, session_key, soul_content, last_seen, last_activity, config, workspace_id, source, hidden, working_memory, taskStats
- Roles map to paralegal specializations: orchestrator (Hermes), litigator, lien-specialist, negotiation, demand, treatment, intake-setup, lead-triage
- Status values: online/offline (currently all offline)
- taskStats embedded: total, assigned, in_progress, quality_review, done, completed

TASKS (/api/tasks):
- 491 tasks currently, all created by FirmVault engine via mc_bridge.py
- Rich model with 35+ fields including: id, title, description, status, priority, assigned_to, created_by, created_at, updated_at, due_date, estimated_hours, actual_hours, tags, metadata (JSON), outcome, error_message, resolution, feedback_rating, feedback_notes, retry_count, completed_at, dispatch_attempts
- GitHub integration fields: github_issue_number, github_repo, github_synced_at, github_branch, github_pr_number, github_pr_state
- Project/ticket system: project_id, project_name, project_prefix, project_ticket_no, ticket_ref (e.g. "TASK-492")

PROJECTS (/api/projects):
- One "General" project with ticket_prefix "TASK", counter at 492
- Fields: id, workspace_id, name, slug, description, ticket_prefix, ticket_counter, status, github_repo, deadline, color, github_sync_enabled

WORKSPACES (/api/workspaces):
- Single "Default Workspace" with tenant_id=1

ACTIVITIES (/api/activities):
- Event log: task_dispatched, task_dispatch_failed, etc.
- Fields: id, type, entity_type, entity_id, actor, description, data, created_at, workspace_id, entity (denormalized)

COMMENTS (/api/tasks/{id}/comments):
- Per-task comment thread (currently empty)

OTHER ENDPOINTS:
- /api/settings — app config (retention policies, etc.)
- /api/webhooks — webhook delivery (empty)
- /api/tokens — token usage tracking
- /api/standup — daily standup generation
- /api/sessions — agent sessions
- /api/skills — skill management (scans disk paths)

3. TASK STATUS WORKFLOW

The MC task model supports these statuses (from the UI i18n strings and API behavior):
- inbox -> assigned -> in_progress -> quality_review -> done/completed
- Also: blocked, failed

Currently ALL 491 tasks are in "assigned" status (bulk-created by FirmVault engine push). The status filter works server-side (?status=inbox, etc.). Bulk updates via PUT /api/tasks with {tasks: [{id, status, ...}]}.

The MC adapter in Hermes defines its own parallel TaskStatus enum:
- PENDING -> ASSIGNED -> IN_PROGRESS -> PENDING_REVIEW -> APPROVED/REJECTED/ERROR

4. HOW HERMES INTERACTS WITH MC

The Hermes daemon (daemon/ directory) is a background thread that runs alongside the Telegram gateway. Architecture:

SUPERVISOR (daemon/supervisor.py) runs two lanes per tick:
  Lane 1: FirmVault pipeline (self-contained: engine -> mc_bridge push -> mc_bridge pull -> git commit)
  Lane 2: GSD + OpenClaw orchestration (poll MC/GSD -> delegate to OpenClaw agents -> collect results -> route to approval)

MC ADAPTER (daemon/adapters/mission_control.py):
- Health check: GET /api/tasks?limit=1
- Heartbeat: Looks up Hermes agent by role="orchestrator" in /api/agents
- Task polling: GET /api/tasks?status=inbox&limit=50 — converts MC tasks to internal Task objects with metadata (case_slug, landmark_id, phase, assigned_to, priority)
- Status updates: Stubbed — "requires Aegis approval" (handled in MC UI)
- Approval routing: Implements ApprovalRouter — checks if task status == "done" via GET /api/tasks/{id}

FIRMVAULT ADAPTER (daemon/adapters/firmvault_pipeline.py):
- Runs the deterministic FirmVault engine (engine.py) to assess all cases
- Uses mc_bridge.py to push available work as MC tasks and pull completed tasks back to update state.yaml
- mc_bridge.py creates tasks with status="inbox", priority based on mandatory flag, metadata containing source="firmvault-engine", case_slug, landmark_id, phase
- Pull reads done tasks from MC, matches case_slug + landmark_id, marks landmarks as satisfied in state.yaml, commits + pushes to git

GSD ADAPTER (daemon/adapters/gsd.py):
- Scans local project dirs for .planning/STATE.md
- Shells out to Node.js bridge (gsd_bridge.mjs) for state reading/writing
- Converts GSD tasks to the same internal Task format
- Status mapping: planned->PENDING, dispatched->ASSIGNED, in_progress->IN_PROGRESS, reviewing->PENDING_REVIEW, done->APPROVED, blocked->REJECTED/ERROR

5. WHAT MC ALREADY PROVIDES AS WORKFLOW/TASK MANAGEMENT

FULLY WORKING:
- Task CRUD (create via POST, read via GET with pagination/filtering, bulk update via PUT)
- Agent registry with role-based assignment
- Project/ticket numbering system (TASK-xxx)
- Activity logging (dispatched, failed, etc.)
- Metadata-rich tasks (JSON metadata field stores source, case_slug, landmark_id, phase, mandatory)
- Comment system per task
- Webhook infrastructure (empty but wired)
- Settings/config management
- Multi-workspace/tenant support
- Status filtering on task queries

PARTIALLY WORKING / INTERFACE ONLY:
- Agent status tracking (all offline — no heartbeat mechanism receiving updates)
- Task dispatch (activity logs show attempts but "spawn openclaw ENOENT" errors)
- Approval workflow (Aegis approval mentioned in adapter but update_task_status is stubbed)
- GitHub sync fields exist on tasks but no sync is active

WHAT'S MISSING:
- No dedicated heartbeat endpoint — MC tracks via last_seen on task operations
- No task assignment/claiming protocol — tasks go straight to "assigned" on creation
- No real-time notifications — daemon polls on a timer
- No distributed locking — documented limitation
- update_task_status in the Hermes adapter is a no-op (status changes "require Aegis approval")
- No mc_bridge.py on disk yet (referenced by FirmVaultAdapter but FIRMVAULT_DIR must have it — and it does at /opt/data/firmvault/skills.tools.workflows/runtime/mc_bridge.py)

6. KEY INTEGRATION POINTS

The data flow is:
FirmVault engine -> mc_bridge.py push -> MC tasks (status=inbox) -> Hermes daemon polls -> delegates to OpenClaw agents -> agent completes -> Hermes collects -> routes to MC approval queue -> mc_bridge.py pull -> state.yaml update -> git commit

MC is the central hub connecting:
- FirmVault (case state/landmarks -> tasks, completed tasks -> landmark satisfaction)
- GSD (ad-hoc project plans -> tasks via daemon)
- OpenClaw agents (task assignment, result collection)
- Hermes (orchestrator: polls, delegates, collects, routes)
- Dashboard UI (human review, approval, monitoring)

7. FILES EXAMINED (no modifications made)

- /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/mission_control.py — MC adapter
- /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/base.py — abstract types (Task, TaskResult, TaskStatus, etc.)
- /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/gsd.py — GSD adapter
- /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/firmvault_pipeline.py — FirmVault pipeline adapter
- /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/openclaw.py — OpenClaw adapter
- /opt/hermes/daemon/supervisor.py — main daemon loop
- /opt/hermes/daemon/poller.py — poll/delegate/collect pipeline
- /opt/hermes/daemon/config.py — env var config
- /opt/hermes/daemon/approval.py — local approval fallback
- /opt/hermes/daemon/health.py — health aggregator
- /opt/hermes/daemon/gsd_bridge.mjs — Node.js GSD bridge
- /opt/hermes/gateway/builtin_hooks/worker_daemon.py — daemon startup hook
- /opt/hermes/docs/worker-daemon.md — comprehensive daemon docs
- /opt/data/firmvault/skills.tools.workflows/runtime/mc_bridge.py — FirmVault<->MC bridge
- /opt/data/firmvault/skills.tools.workflows/runtime/engine.py — FirmVault state machine engine
- Live API probing of all MC endpoints
